# 'branch merge' refuses to merge branches
# with unpushed local commits.

as 'Test <test@example.com>'
at '2024-04-05T16:40:32Z'

# setup
cd repo
git init
git commit --allow-empty -m 'Initial commit'

# set up a fake GitHub remote
shamhub init
shamhub new origin alice/example.git
shamhub register alice
git push origin main

env SHAMHUB_USERNAME=alice
gs auth login

# create a stack: main -> feature1 -> feature2
git add feature1.txt
gs bc feature1 -m 'Add feature 1'
git add feature2.txt
gs bc feature2 -m 'Add feature 2'

# submit the stack (pushes to remote)
gs downstack submit --fill
stderr 'Created #1'
stderr 'Created #2'

# add an unpushed commit on feature1
gs bco feature1
cp $WORK/extra.txt extra.txt
git add extra.txt
git commit -m 'Add extra (not pushed)'
gs bco feature2

# merge should fail because feature1 has unpushed commits
! gs branch merge
stderr 'out of sync'
stderr 'feature1'
stderr 'gs branch submit'
stderr 'git reset --hard'

-- repo/feature1.txt --
This is feature 1
-- repo/feature2.txt --
This is feature 2
-- extra.txt --
This is an unpushed change
